description_long: This object is a classic desktop computer setup featuring a CRT monitor, a tower-style CPU, a full-sized keyboard, and a wired mouse. The monitor has a boxy, deep profile with a thick, light gray bezel framing a black screen displaying green text, typical of older computer displays. The rear of the monitor is vented and protrudes significantly, with visible cable connections. The CPU tower is rectangular, with multiple drive bays and a vented rear panel, and is also light gray in color. The keyboard is standard, with a full set of keys, and the mouse is a simple, rounded design, both connected by visible cables. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on function over form, and the materials appear to be primarily plastic and metal.
description: A vintage desktop computer setup with a CRT monitor, tower CPU, keyboard, and mouse, all in light gray plastic with visible cables and a boxy, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the CRT monitor's thick bezel, black screen with green text, and the full keyboard and mouse in front of the tower CPU.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the deep, boxy profile of the CRT monitor and the slimness of the keyboard and mouse.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the vented back of the monitor, the rear panel of the CPU tower, and the cable connections between components.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view emphasizing the monitor's protruding back and the arrangement of the keyboard and mouse.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and light gray, with squared corners and a uniform width around the display area. It appears to be made of matte plastic with no decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a wide, flat rectangular base made of matching light gray plastic, providing stability without visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: A power button and possibly other control buttons are located on the lower right front bezel of the monitor, grouped together and slightly raised for tactile feedback.
